text: Code reviews are an essential part of software development. During a code review, a developer who did not write the code carefully examines it before it becomes part of the main project. This process is highly valuable because it helps catch bugs early. Finding and fixing a problem at this stage is far cheaper and easier than discovering it after the software is released. Beyond finding errors, code reviews also promote knowledge sharing. Team members learn new techniques and understand different parts of the project better. Ultimately, this practice improves the overall team quality by creating a shared standard of excellence and encouraging collaboration.
